data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Battery Minerals Ltd. ( (AU:WTM) ).

Waratah Minerals has reported a change in director Naomi Scott’s holdings, confirming the lapse and cancellation of a series of unlisted options after their exercise period expired. Scott disposed of 66,667 unlisted options exercisable at $1.20 and 66,667 unlisted options at $1.65, both with a 31 March 2026 expiry, leaving her with ordinary shares and Class B performance rights only.

The company stated that no value or consideration was received for the expired options, as they were simply cancelled upon expiry and not traded. The notice clarifies that the transactions did not occur during a closed period and therefore did not require prior written clearance, signaling a routine cleanup of unexercised director incentives rather than an active on-market change in ownership.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WTM) stock is a Buy with a A$0.95 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Battery Minerals Ltd. stock, see the AU:WTM Stock Forecast page.

More about Battery Minerals Ltd.

Waratah Minerals Limited is an Australian-listed resources company focused on mineral exploration and development. The company’s securities include ordinary shares, unlisted options and performance rights traded on the ASX, reflecting its positioning within the mining and minerals sector.

data.title: Saferoads Delivers Solid Quarter as Key Customer Sales Exceed Commitments
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Saferoads Holdings Limited ( (AU:SRH) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Saferoads reported third-quarter sales of $2.9 million, slightly below the prior quarter due to the Christmas shutdown, but maintained a positive operating cash flow of $79,000 and an unaudited quarterly profit of $62,000, bringing year-to-date profit to $566,000. Sales to key customer On-site Rentals reached $1.18 million for the quarter and $4.84 million year-to-date, now exceeding OSR’s original post–business-sale purchase commitment by $2 million, underscoring the strategic importance of this relationship.

The company is progressing several product improvement initiatives, including cyclone-rated portable solar lights, a 1.5-metre Rapid Stop pedestrian barrier, upgraded variable message sign designs and the pinned HV2 product, all aimed at boosting future product sales and competitiveness. Management highlighted ongoing challenges for the remainder of the financial year, notably rising costs for oil-based inputs such as fuel and plastics, the need to further grow market share and uncertainty over future order volumes from On-site Rentals, factors that could influence margins and growth trajectory.

More about Saferoads Holdings Limited

Saferoads Holdings Limited operates in the road safety and traffic management industry, supplying products such as portable solar lighting, pedestrian protection barriers, variable message signs and other safety systems. The company focuses on enhancing its product range and leveraging a strengthened sales team to grow market share, including significant sales to rental sector customers such as On-site Rentals.

Average Trading Volume: 68,802

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$3.76M

data.title: Marlin Global reshapes board as Fiona Oliver named chair and Simon Flood joins as director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Marlin Global Ltd. ( (NZ:MLN) ).

Marlin Global Limited has refreshed its boardroom, appointing seasoned investment management executive Simon Flood as an independent director from 1 June 2026. Flood brings international leadership experience from London, Hong Kong, Singapore and New Zealand, and will also serve on the boards of sister funds Kingfish Limited and Barramundi Limited.

The company also confirmed a planned chair transition, with current chair Andy Coupe set to retire on 30 June 2026 and existing independent director Fiona Oliver to assume the role of chair from that date. The board emphasized that Flood’s appointment complies with the company’s constitution and NZX requirements, and he will stand for shareholder election at the November 2026 annual meeting, reinforcing governance continuity for investors.

More about Marlin Global Ltd.

Marlin Global Limited is a New Zealand-based listed investment company focused on global equities, providing investors with exposure to international markets. The firm operates under NZX Listing Rules and shares governance structures with related vehicles Kingfish Limited and Barramundi Limited, reflecting a broader platform in listed investment funds.

Lachlan Star ( (AU:LSA) ) has issued an update.

Lachlan Star Limited has issued a notice convening a general meeting of shareholders to be held on 28 May 2026 in West Perth, Western Australia. The company has outlined voting procedures, including eligibility cut-off, in-person participation, and proxy appointment rules, underscoring the importance of shareholder engagement in upcoming resolutions.

Shareholders registered by the specified date will be entitled to vote, either directly at the meeting or through one or two appointed proxies who do not need to be company members. Narryer Metals Limited has raised $931,000 through the issue of 31,033,334 fully paid ordinary shares at $0.03 per share in a Tranche 1 placement, issued without a prospectus under the Corporations Act. ENIGMO, Inc. ( (JP:3665) ) has shared an update.

Enigmo Inc., an equity-method affiliate of Sony Group Corporation, clarified that while Sony holds 25.2% of its voting rights, the company independently determines its management policies and business strategy. Enigmo also has one outside director from Sony, appointed to reinforce its management structure with broader perspectives and experience-based advice.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from GenusPlus Group Ltd. ( (AU:GNP) ) is now available.

GenusPlus Group Ltd, a national provider of essential power and communications infrastructure across Australia, focuses on designing, building and maintaining transmission and distribution networks, substations and battery systems. It also delivers integrated communication network solutions, enabling customers to adopt new generation technologies and meet the demands of a carbon-neutral economy.

The company has secured a lump-sum, turn-key contract worth about $110 million to deliver the balance-of-plant scope and battery energy storage system installation for the 200MW/800MWh Koolunga Battery Energy Storage System project in South Australia. Work on the state-significant KLG project, owned by Equis Development, will begin shortly and is expected to complete by September 2027, reinforcing GenusPlus’s capability in large, complex energy storage infrastructure and its role in Australia’s clean energy transition.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:GNP) stock is a Buy with a A$9.49 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GenusPlus Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:GNP Stock Forecast page.

More about GenusPlus Group Ltd.

GenusPlus Group Ltd is an Australian specialist in power and communications infrastructure and services. The company designs, builds and maintains electrical transmission and distribution networks, substations and battery systems, and helps customers integrate new generation technologies and scalable communication networks to support a carbon-neutral economy.

data.title: Essential Properties Realty Trust Lifts Guidance After Strong Q1
data.description:
Essential Properties Realty Trust ((EPRT)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Essential Properties Realty Trust’s latest earnings call carried an upbeat tone, as management pointed to double‑digit AFFO per share growth, strong capital deployment, and a fortified balance sheet. While they acknowledged a few idiosyncratic credit events and softer trends in certain restaurant and entertainment segments, leadership stressed that these were contained against broadly healthy portfolio metrics.

Strong quarterly earnings and AFFO growth

Essential Properties opened the quarter with momentum, posting GAAP net income of $60.0 million and AFFO of $105.8 million. AFFO per share reached $0.50, rising 11% year over year and underscoring solid earnings expansion in what remains a choppy macro backdrop.

Robust capital deployment and equity raises

The REIT stayed firmly on offense, deploying $389 million into 126 properties during the first quarter, signaling confidence in its acquisition pipeline. To support this growth, the company raised about $419 million of equity, including a large overnight deal, and ended the period with $541 million of unsettled forward equity still to be deployed.

Raised full-year guidance

Reflecting this strong start, management lifted 2026 AFFO per share guidance to a range of $2.00 to $2.05, implying around 7% growth at the midpoint. They also stepped up full‑year investment volume expectations by $100 million, now targeting between $1.1 billion and $1.5 billion of acquisitions.

Healthy balance sheet and liquidity

Balance sheet strength remained a central theme, with available liquidity of $1.5 billion providing ample dry powder for future deals. Pro forma net debt to annualized adjusted EBITDAre stood at just 3.5x, while income‑producing gross assets surpassed $7.5 billion, underscoring the scale and financial flexibility of the platform.

High-quality portfolio metrics

The company’s portfolio spanned 2,417 properties leased to more than 400 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of roughly 15 years. Near‑term lease rollover risk appeared limited, with just 2.8% of annual base rent expiring over the next three years and occupancy running at a robust 99.7%.

Positive same-store and credit trends

Same‑store rent grew 1.4% in the quarter, showing steady organic expansion from built‑in escalators. Rent coverage across the portfolio remained strong at 3.5x, and the share of annual base rent under 1.5x coverage fell by 140 basis points, suggesting incremental improvement in tenant credit quality.

Attractive investment yield and underwriting

On new investments, Essential Properties continued to lock in compelling economics, reporting an initial cap rate of 7.7% and a GAAP yield of 8.8%. These deals came with a weighted average initial lease term of 17.7 years, average rent bumps of 2.1%, and unit‑level rent coverage around 3.1x, indicating disciplined underwriting at favorable spreads to its cost of capital.

Notable Denny’s portfolio acquisition

One highlight of the quarter was the acquisition of 74 Denny’s restaurants for $147 million, or under $2 million per property. Management framed the deal as an example of relationship‑driven sourcing and a granular asset profile that should be easier to re‑tenant, supporting both risk management and long‑term cash flow durability.

Cost discipline and cash generation

Expenses remained well‑controlled, with total G&A at $12.3 million and cash G&A at $8.0 million, or about 5.0% of total revenue, down from 5.9% a year earlier. The company now expects slightly lower cash G&A for the year and generated roughly $40 million of free cash flow after dividends in the quarter, or about $160 million on an annualized basis.

Shareholder returns and modest payout

Investors saw steady income, with the board declaring a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share. That payment represented a conservative AFFO payout ratio of roughly 62%, leaving meaningful retained cash flow to reinvest in acquisitions and support continued per‑share growth.

Operator-specific bankruptcy and tenant credit event

Not everything was smooth, as one restaurant operator filed for bankruptcy, affecting seven properties and about 30 basis points of annual base rent. Management expects to recover roughly 80% of that exposure through backfills and sales, but the situation required active asset management and highlighted the importance of tenant scrutiny.

Quarterly impairment driven by home furnishings tenant

The company also recorded impairment charges tied primarily to a former home furnishings location previously impacted by a tenant bankruptcy and lease rejection. While the impairment, cited around $16 million, was meaningful at the asset level, management emphasized that it remained isolated within an otherwise stable and diversified portfolio.

Pressure in casual dining & entertainment verticals

Executives flagged ongoing softness in casual dining and entertainment, where sales were generally flat to down 2% to 3% and margins compressed by about 100 to 200 basis points. They estimated this could translate into roughly 10 to 20 basis points of pressure on rent coverage in those specific sub‑sectors, though the overall portfolio impact remains modest.

Potential equity dilution from unsettled forwards

Looking at the capital structure, unsettled forward equity of $541 million at an average price of $30.55 represents a future source of funding but also some dilution risk. Management already builds a conservative $0.01 to $0.02 per‑share impact into full‑year AFFO guidance, and these forward shares are reflected in the diluted share count.

Early 2Q transaction pace uncertainty

The company cautioned that the second quarter started slowly on the deal front and that acquisitions may lag the first quarter’s robust pace. They are modeling 2Q acquisition volume in the $275 million to $325 million range, introducing some timing uncertainty even as the full‑year investment targets move higher.

Moderated disposition volume and idiosyncratic sales timing

On the disposition side, sales activity moderated to $10.2 million in the quarter at a 6.9% cap rate, following a heavier sell‑down late last year. Management reiterated that dispositions are primarily a tool for portfolio shaping and that timing can be lumpy, rather than a consistent driver of accretive capital recycling.

Forward-looking guidance and growth outlook

Guidance for 2026 paints a constructive picture, with AFFO per share expected between $2.00 and $2.05 and acquisition volume guided to $1.1 billion to $1.5 billion, supported by cap rates in the mid‑to‑high 7% range. With low leverage, $1.5 billion of liquidity, modest expected dilution, and about $160 million of annual retained cash flow, management appears confident it can fund growth while maintaining a disciplined cost structure.

Essential Properties Realty Trust’s call framed a company leaning into growth while keeping risk in check, backed by strong earnings, a conservative balance sheet, and high‑quality leases. For investors, the story centers on visible AFFO expansion, attractive acquisition economics, and a prudent payout, with the main watch points being isolated credit issues and the pace of deal execution over the next few quarters.
